@@ -0,0 +1,83 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Spatial focus invariant: something is always focused; nav key on null picks a sensible default'
+---
+## What
+
+**Invariant:** whenever at least one `FocusScope` is registered in the active layer, the focused moniker is non-null. The user must never end up "stuck with nothing focused" and unable to navigate.
+
+The user reproduces this reliably from the LeftNav: click a view-switcher button → the previous view unmounts and its focused scope's rect is unregistered → the Rust store clears `focused_key` to None → React's `focusedMoniker` goes null → subsequent `h/j/k/l` keys do nothing because `broadcastNavCommand` short-circuits on `if (!focusedMk) return false`.
+
+This task enforces the invariant at two layers and adds a nav-key safety net so the user can always recover.
+
+### Root cause evidence
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:365-378` — `broadcastNavCommand` returns `false` when `focusedMk` is null. No fallback.
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:356-362` (`unregister()`) — clears `focused_key` to `None` if the unregistering scope was focused, with no successor pick.
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:524-545` (`navigate()`) — returns `Ok(None)` when the source key is unknown/missing. There's a `fallbackToFirst()` path documented but it doesn't cover this case today.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx:157-159` — `handleClick` does `setFocus(mk)` then `dispatch("view.switch:...")`; the ensuing unmount can unregister the focused scope before the view remount re-registers anything, leaving a transient null.
+
+### Approach — two mechanisms, one invariant
+
+#### Mechanism 1: auto-pick a successor on unregister (Rust)
+
+When `SpatialState::unregister(key)` is called and `key == focused_key`, pick a replacement in priority order:
+
+1. **Layer focus memory** — if the current layer has a `last_focused` entry that's still registered and not the one being removed, use it.
+2. **Sibling in same `parent_scope`** — if the unregistering entry has a `parent_scope`, pick the first registered sibling (lowest ordinal / top-left rect) in that parent.
+3. **First-in-layer by position** — topmost-leftmost registered entry in the active layer (the same rule the "first" direction uses).
+4. **None** — only if the active layer is genuinely empty.
+
+Emit a `focus-changed` event when the successor is picked, so React re-syncs.
+
+Files: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs`, `kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (unregister command — may need to forward the new event).
+
+#### Mechanism 2: nav-key safety net (Rust + JS)
+
+When a nav key fires and the source moniker is null OR references an unregistered key, treat the call as "pick a first" — the same path `Direction::First` takes today. Return that entry as the new focused key and emit `focus-changed`.
+
+Currently `navigate()` returns `Ok(None)` on unknown source. Change it to fall through to first-in-layer on null/stale source, via the existing `fallbackToFirst()` helper.
+
+On the JS side, drop the `if (!focusedMk) return false` short-circuit in `broadcastNavCommand` — always invoke `spatial_navigate`, passing the current (possibly null) moniker. Let Rust decide the successor.
+
+Files: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` (navigate fallback), `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` (drop the short-circuit).
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` — successor-pick in `unregister()`, fallback-on-null-or-stale in `navigate()`
+- `kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` — only if the event-emit path needs a shape change
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — remove the `if (!focusedMk) return false` gate in `broadcastNavCommand`
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — port the same successor-pick + null-source-fallback logic so JS parity tests stay green
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — new cases: unregister-focused-scope, navigate-with-null-source, navigate-with-stale-source
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] While at least one scope is registered in the active layer, `focused_moniker` is never null after any sequence of register/unregister/navigate calls
+- [ ] When the currently-focused scope is unregistered, Rust picks a successor using the priority order (layer memory → sibling → first-in-layer) and emits `focus-changed`
+- [ ] When a nav key fires with `focused_moniker` null, Rust selects the first-in-layer entry and emits `focus-changed` (same for stale/unregistered source moniker)
+- [ ] LeftNav reproduction: focus any body cell, click a LeftNav button to switch views, press any nav key → focus lands on a registered scope in the new view (not a no-op)
+- [ ] Inspector close: open inspector, focus an inspector row, close the inspector → focus lands on a window-layer scope (not null)
+- [ ] Empty-layer edge case: if the only layer has zero registered scopes, `focused_moniker` is null and nav keys are a no-op (no crash, no loop)
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Rust: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` unit tests — add three cases:
+  - `unregister_focused_picks_layer_memory_first`
+  - `unregister_focused_picks_sibling_when_no_memory`
+  - `unregister_focused_picks_first_in_layer_as_last_resort`
+- [ ] Rust: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for navigate-with-null-source and navigate-with-stale-source so Rust and JS agree
+- [ ] JS: `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.test.tsx` (or adjacent) — assert `broadcastNavCommand` invokes `spatial_navigate` even when the focused moniker is null, and the resulting `focus-changed` event populates the moniker store
+- [ ] JS: `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` (or the existing leftnav test) — add a case: focus a body cell, click a LeftNav button, verify focus lands on a scope in the new view (not null)
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — all new tests green
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ tests still pass
+- [ ] Manual: the user's reproduction case — click LeftNav buttons repeatedly, press nav keys between clicks, focus never goes null
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — start with the Rust unit tests in `spatial_state.rs` (fastest feedback), then the parity cases (Rust + JS), then the JS integration test.
+- Successor-pick logic must be in Rust (source of truth). JS shim mirrors it through parity tests.
+

changes.value: Spatial focus invariant: something is always focused; nav key on null picks a sensible default

## What

**Invariant:** whenever at least one `FocusScope` is registered in the active layer, the focused moniker is non-null. The user must never end up "stuck with nothing focused" and unable to navigate.

The user reproduces this reliably from the LeftNav: click a view-switcher button → the previous view unmounts and its focused scope's rect is unregistered → the Rust store clears `focused_key` to None → React's `focusedMoniker` goes null → subsequent `h/j/k/l` keys do nothing because `broadcastNavCommand` short-circuits on `if (!focusedMk) return false`.

This task enforces the invariant at two layers and adds a nav-key safety net so the user can always recover.

### Root cause evidence

- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:365-378` — `broadcastNavCommand` returns `false` when `focusedMk` is null. No fallback.
- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:356-362` (`unregister()`) — clears `focused_key` to `None` if the unregistering scope was focused, with no successor pick.
- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:524-545` (`navigate()`) — returns `Ok(None)` when the source key is unknown/missing. There's a `fallbackToFirst()` path documented but it doesn't cover this case today.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx:157-159` — `handleClick` does `setFocus(mk)` then `dispatch("view.switch:...")`; the ensuing unmount can unregister the focused scope before the view remount re-registers anything, leaving a transient null.

### Approach — two mechanisms, one invariant

#### Mechanism 1: auto-pick a successor on unregister (Rust)

When `SpatialState::unregister(key)` is called and `key == focused_key`, pick a replacement in priority order:

1. **Layer focus memory** — if the current layer has a `last_focused` entry that's still registered and not the one being removed, use it.
2. **Sibling in same `parent_scope`** — if the unregistering entry has a `parent_scope`, pick the first registered sibling (lowest ordinal / top-left rect) in that parent.
3. **First-in-layer by position** — topmost-leftmost registered entry in the active layer (the same rule the "first" direction uses).
4. **None** — only if the active layer is genuinely empty.

Emit a `focus-changed` event when the successor is picked, so React re-syncs.

Files: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs`, `kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (unregister command — may need to forward the new event).

#### Mechanism 2: nav-key safety net (Rust + JS)

When a nav key fires and the source moniker is null OR references an unregistered key, treat the call as "pick a first" — the same path `Direction::First` takes today. Return that entry as the new focused key and emit `focus-changed`.

Currently `navigate()` returns `Ok(None)` on unknown source. Change it to fall through to first-in-layer on null/stale source, via the existing `fallbackToFirst()` helper.

On the JS side, drop the `if (!focusedMk) return false` short-circuit in `broadcastNavCommand` — always invoke `spatial_navigate`, passing the current (possibly null) moniker. Let Rust decide the successor.

Files: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` (navigate fallback), `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` (drop the short-circuit).

### Files to modify

- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` — successor-pick in `unregister()`, fallback-on-null-or-stale in `navigate()`
- `kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` — only if the event-emit path needs a shape change
- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — remove the `if (!focusedMk) return false` gate in `broadcastNavCommand`
- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — port the same successor-pick + null-source-fallback logic so JS parity tests stay green
- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — new cases: unregister-focused-scope, navigate-with-null-source, navigate-with-stale-source

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] While at least one scope is registered in the active layer, `focused_moniker` is never null after any sequence of register/unregister/navigate calls
- [ ] When the currently-focused scope is unregistered, Rust picks a successor using the priority order (layer memory → sibling → first-in-layer) and emits `focus-changed`
- [ ] When a nav key fires with `focused_moniker` null, Rust selects the first-in-layer entry and emits `focus-changed` (same for stale/unregistered source moniker)
- [ ] LeftNav reproduction: focus any body cell, click a LeftNav button to switch views, press any nav key → focus lands on a registered scope in the new view (not a no-op)
- [ ] Inspector close: open inspector, focus an inspector row, close the inspector → focus lands on a window-layer scope (not null)
- [ ] Empty-layer edge case: if the only layer has zero registered scopes, `focused_moniker` is null and nav keys are a no-op (no crash, no loop)

## Tests

- [ ] Rust: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` unit tests — add three cases:
  - `unregister_focused_picks_layer_memory_first`
  - `unregister_focused_picks_sibling_when_no_memory`
  - `unregister_focused_picks_first_in_layer_as_last_resort`
- [ ] Rust: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for navigate-with-null-source and navigate-with-stale-source so Rust and JS agree
- [ ] JS: `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.test.tsx` (or adjacent) — assert `broadcastNavCommand` invokes `spatial_navigate` even when the focused moniker is null, and the resulting `focus-changed` event populates the moniker store
- [ ] JS: `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` (or the existing leftnav test) — add a case: focus a body cell, click a LeftNav button, verify focus lands on a scope in the new view (not null)
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — all new tests green
- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ tests still pass
- [ ] Manual: the user's reproduction case — click LeftNav buttons repeatedly, press nav keys between clicks, focus never goes null

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — start with the Rust unit tests in `spatial_state.rs` (fastest feedback), then the parity cases (Rust + JS), then the JS integration test.
- Successor-pick logic must be in Rust (source of truth). JS shim mirrors it through parity tests.

--- original

@@ -14,46 +14,6 @@

 This task enforces the invariant at two layers and adds a nav-key safety net so the user can always recover.

-### Root cause evidence
-
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:365-378` — `broadcastNavCommand` returns `false` when `focusedMk` is null. No fallback.
-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:356-362` (`unregister()`) — clears `focused_key` to `None` if the unregistering scope was focused, with no successor pick.
-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs:524-545` (`navigate()`) — returns `Ok(None)` when the source key is unknown/missing. There's a `fallbackToFirst()` path documented but it doesn't cover this case today.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx:157-159` — `handleClick` does `setFocus(mk)` then `dispatch("view.switch:...")`; the ensuing unmount can unregister the focused scope before the view remount re-registers anything, leaving a transient null.
-
-### Approach — two mechanisms, one invariant
-
-#### Mechanism 1: auto-pick a successor on unregister (Rust)
-
-When `SpatialState::unregister(key)` is called and `key == focused_key`, pick a replacement in priority order:
-
-1. **Layer focus memory** — if the current layer has a `last_focused` entry that's still registered and not the one being removed, use it.
-2. **Sibling in same `parent_scope`** — if the unregistering entry has a `parent_scope`, pick the first registered sibling (lowest ordinal / top-left rect) in that parent.
-3. **First-in-layer by position** — topmost-leftmost registered entry in the active layer (the same rule the "first" direction uses).
-4. **None** — only if the active layer is genuinely empty.
-
-Emit a `focus-changed` event when the successor is picked, so React re-syncs.
-
-Files: `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs`, `kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (unregister command — may need to forward the new event).
